issues.description: ADR-001 uses two extra sections ('Why Needed' and 'Boundary Preserved') beyond the standard template (Status/Context/Decision/Alternatives Rejected/Consequences/Cross-references). While these extra sections are functionally useful and required by the allowlist evidence checker (check-allowlist-change-evidence.sh), the INDEX.md template section only documents the six standard sections. This could confuse future ADR authors who follow the template strictly.

ADR-001 lacks the standard 'Context' section header content that the other ADRs have. It uses 'Context' but then also adds 'Why Needed' as a separate section, which partially overlaps in purpose with the standard Context section. A future cleanup could merge 'Why Needed' into 'Context' while keeping the extra 'Boundary Preserved' section as an allowlist-specific addition documented in the INDEX.md template.

sharedStateObservations.observation: ADR-001 has a slightly different section structure than ADRs 002-006 because it must satisfy the allowlist evidence checker (check-allowlist-change-evidence.sh) which requires '## Why Needed', '## Alternatives Rejected', and '## Boundary Preserved' headings. The INDEX.md ADR template only lists the six standard sections and does not mention allowlist-specific extensions. Future workers creating allowlist ADRs may not know they need the extra sections unless they read the shell script.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: INDEX.md template section lists Status/Context/Decision/Alternatives Rejected/Consequences/Cross-references. check-allowlist-change-evidence.sh (line ~59) requires '## Why Needed', '## Alternatives Rejected', '## Boundary Preserved'. ADR-001 includes both the standard and extra sections.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ADR directory was originally docs/adr/ (singular) for ADR-001, then docs/adrs/ (plural) for ADRs 002-006. The worker fixed this by copying ADR-001 into docs/adrs/ and updating the CI script to accept both paths. However, the original docs/adr/ directory may still exist as a source of confusion. The convention is now docs/adrs/ (plural) but this is not explicitly stated in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ci/architecture/check-allowlist-change-evidence.sh was updated (commit 80b9e1c5) to match 'docs/adr[s]?/' — accepting both singular and plural. ADR-001 was copied to docs/adrs/ to unify.

codeReview.summary: The feature successfully seeds the ADR index and six accepted ADRs covering all five required platform decisions (multi-tenant auth scoping, outbox/idempotency, audit layering, migration posture, portal/host boundaries) plus a bonus ADR for cross-module import edges. All ADRs are grounded in actual codebase evidence, reference real classes and services, and explain current architecture rather than future aspirations. The docs/INDEX.md is properly updated with an ADR section linking to the ADR index.

issues.description: Contains extra sections ('Why Needed', 'Boundary Preserved') beyond the standard template documented in INDEX.md. These are required by the allowlist evidence checker but not documented in the ADR template guidance.

summary: PASS — The docs-foundation-adr-seed feature delivers a complete, well-structured ADR index and six accepted ADRs covering all required platform decisions. Each ADR follows a consistent Status/Context/Decision/Alternatives Rejected/Consequences/Cross-references format, contains evidence grounded in real codebase classes and services, and explains current rather than aspirational architecture. Coverage is complete: multi-tenant auth scoping (ADR-002), outbox/idempotency (ADR-003), audit layering (ADR-004), migration posture (ADR-005), and portal/host boundaries (ADR-006). ADR-001 is a bonus covering cross-module import edges. Minor non-blocking issues: ADR-001 has extra sections required by the allowlist checker but not documented in the INDEX.md template, and the singular/plural directory split (docs/adr/ vs docs/adrs/) was resolved but the convention isn't documented in AGENTS.md.
